reasoning: The TGA baseline is currently around $316B (as of Mar 26, 2025), artificially suppressed as the Treasury uses extraordinary measures following the Jan 2, 2025 debt ceiling reinstatement. The TGA has been draining steadily ($50-$100k/week) to fund obligations. By April 6, it is highly likely the balance will have continued this drain prior to tax day (lowering probability to 0.15). However, by April 29, mid-April tax receipts historically inject massive liquidity into the TGA, making an increase highly likely (0.82). For the longer-term dates (June, September, and March 2026), the debt limit dispute will either be resolved (triggering a massive Treasury rebuild of the TGA back to its ~$700B+ target) or still be depleting. A resolution is the most likely long-term outcome, implying the TGA will ultimately settle much higher than the current ~316B depressed baseline. Per system instructions, I have strictly scaled uncertainty such that probabilities regress toward 0.5 as the forecast horizon extends (distances to 0.5 are 0.35, 0.32, 0.25, 0.18, 0.10 respectively).
